title: Portal Review Bundle (what the clinician reviewer was shown, hashed)
description: The complete review workspace presented to a clinician in the Verification Portal (portal/review-bundle.js): the exact candidate output + medicolegal hash, verification results, receipts, evidence claims, firewall status, and the producer-gated audit artifacts (PPP-TTT verdict, ABCDE record, history summary, conflict audit). bundle_sha256 is computed over the canonical bundle-without-that-field and is recorded with the clinician's decision (gate-record-store), so the audit trail proves what was REVIEWED, not just what was decided. Zod mirror: portal/review-bundle-schema exports in portal/review-bundle.js (this JSON schema is the source of truth).

dose_evidence.description: THE EVIDENCE PLANE (E3, R-47b). Everything the system holds about a dose, assembled for the CLINICIAN and rendered in the surface they review. Advisory by default and provenance-tagged: the clinician-signed AU dose, the US/EU approved labels beside it VERBATIM, a CDS gateway's dose candidate as a second executor's opinion, what the primary literature reports, the congruence appraisal and the plausibility read. WHY IT IS HERE AND NOT IN PharmCheck: 'no autonomous prescription' means the AI must not MINT a dose; it does not mean a registered practitioner may not be SHOWN one with its provenance. PharmCheck.dose_guidance (frozen) stays the clinician-signed AU dose ALONE and is the only patient-promotable dose. These items are never patient-facing: patient_facing is const false, and the bar between the planes is portal/verification-gate.js releaseToPatient(). This array is inside bundle_sha256, so 'the clinician saw the divergence' — the precondition the AU-primacy ruling assumes — becomes provable rather than presumed.

kind.description: What this item IS. Only au_dose_signed can ever be authoritative.

authority.description: 'authoritative' is reserved for a clinician-ATTESTED AU dose (review_status:approved). Everything else — foreign labels, CDS candidates, literature, appraisals — is advisory: it informs the practitioner's decision and never substitutes for it. An authored-but-unattested AU dose is advisory, not authoritative.

jurisdiction.description: Where this evidence has force. A foreign label is NEVER an AU dose.

text.description: The VERBATIM thing the clinician reads. Never paraphrased — the agent may segment and label, never rewrite.

status.description: Kind-specific state: authorisation_status (ACTIVE/WITHDRAWN), congruence (congruent/non_congruent/no_comparator), plausibility (plausible/implausible/unassessable), or queue_status.

source.description: Provenance — where this came from. No item without one.

attested_by.description: The registered practitioner who attested it, when attested.

entered_by.description: AHPRA registration id on the clinician authoring channel.

population.description: The study population — a literature dose may not transfer to this patient.

citation.description: The retrieval-verified citation for a literature item.

evidence_note.description: Design limits of the evidence (confounding, observational, etc.).

note.description: How to read this item — its limits, stated in the clinician's terms.

patient_facing.description: MECHANICAL. Evidence-plane items are clinician-facing only. Nothing here reaches a patient except through the clinician's gate decision (releaseToPatient), which binds to the exact output bytes.
